message:
fix(cli): rename --force to --yes across all user-facing recovery messages and add CLI alias

Consistency fix: all recovery/repair guidance strings now reference
`--yes` instead of the old `--force` flag, matching the actual CLI
argument name.  This affects robot status output, startup check
messages, and identity tool responses.

Additionally:
- Add `--force` as a hidden alias for `--yes` on `doctor repair` so
  users following old instructions still work (alias = "force")
- Suppress launchctl stderr noise: use .output() instead of .status()
  so "Could not find service" messages don't leak to the terminal when
  the macOS launch agent isn't loaded
- Add informational messages about health probe noise: when checking
  whether a port hosts an Agent Mail server, the probe can cause the
  old server to emit "Bad request." — these comments and eprintln
  notes explain that the output is harmless
- Add retry with backoff for integrity probe activity lock: after
  auto_clear_db_blockers kills a stale process, the kernel may need
  a few hundred ms to release flock() descriptors; retries up to 8
  times with 150ms backoff instead of failing immediately

Files changed:
- crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/lib.rs
- crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/robot.rs (+ test string updates)
- crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/startup_checks.rs
- crates/mcp-agent-mail-tools/src/identity.rs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
